description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane with a green fuselage and wings, accented by two broad orange diagonal stripes on the upper wing. The aircraft features two sets of straight, parallel wings connected by black struts and wires, giving it a classic double-decker appearance. The nose houses a single propeller, and the cockpit is positioned just behind the engine, likely open and single-seat. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear appears fixed and simple, with no visible wheel fairings. The overall design suggests a lightweight, maneuverable aircraft typical of early 20th-century aviation.
description: A green and orange vintage biplane with double wings, a single propeller, and a classic open cockpit. The aircraft has a conventional tail and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular nose, single propeller, and the stacked biplane wings with orange stripes.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the green upper wing with two orange diagonal stripes and the narrow fuselage.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, including the single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
description_view_3: Bottom-up side view showing the lower wing, struts, and the underside of the fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings; no winglets or noticeable dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the engine.
